gene_region.label: FLG transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical FLG genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup for ENSG00000143631 on the primary assembly, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window relative to the reverse-strand transcription start, used to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: FLG encodes filaggrin, a key epidermal barrier protein whose loss-of-function variants are strongly linked to ichthyosis vulgaris, atopic dermatitis, allergic sensitization, and asthma risk in barrier-impaired contexts.
gene_context.clinical_context: The bundled FLG knowledge base is focused on asthma, allergy, atopic dermatitis, airway inflammation, epithelial-barrier biology, and immune-regulatory research. Seeded markers and locus models are intended as research context and should not be promoted to diagnostic, prognostic, or treatment-selection claims without external clinical curation.
variant_effect_overview: FLG loss-of-function alleles are higher-impact barrier-disruption markers than most common allergic-disease GWAS SNPs.
Asthma interpretation is usually strongest when atopic dermatitis, eczema, or barrier dysfunction is also present.
condition_research_overview: Allergic sensitization and atopic asthma context
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: FLG methylation should be read as local regulatory context around an allergic-inflammation gene. Promoter-proximal or gene-body beta values may help frame accessibility or expression hypotheses, but they are not validated stand-alone biomarkers for asthma, eczema, allergy, immune activation, or treatment response.
methylation_effects: Promoter-proximal methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local regulatory state for FLG expression potential.
Interpret methylation with tissue, cell composition, environmental exposure, disease state, medication history, and genotype context.
methylation_condition_research: Asthma, allergy, atopic dermatitis, eosinophilic inflammation, and airway epithelial studies.
Immune-cell, epithelial-barrier, cytokine-signaling, and inflammatory-disease epigenetic research.

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000143631: FLG coordinates and transcripts

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: rs61816761 (FLG R501X) is bundled as a FLG research marker for allergic disease, asthma, eczema, airway inflammation, or immune-regulatory cohort literature. Treat it as low-penetrance, cohort-aware association context rather than as a diagnostic or treatment-selection allele.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Clinically important FLG loss-of-function research and barrier-risk marker; classification still depends on zygosity and external clinical review.
functional_effects: May tag regulatory, expression, coding, haplotype, or linked-locus variation around FLG.
Reported effects should be interpreted with ancestry, phenotype definition, tissue, and genotype dosage in mind.
associated_conditions: Allergic sensitization and atopic asthma context
research_context: Use this marker as a research context flag and not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Direction and effect size can vary across asthma, eczema, allergy, immune, epithelial, and ancestry-specific cohorts.
Confirm exact allele, build, zygosity, QC, and LD/haplotype context before drawing marker-level conclusions.
variant_records.usual_variant_note: FLG marker used for allergic-inflammation research context.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: FLG methylation can provide local regulatory context, but it does not establish the variant, haplotype, expression state, or phenotype direction for rs61816761 (FLG R501X).

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000143631: FLG coordinates and transcripts

literature_findings.paper: Asthma, allergy, and immune-trait GWAS and functional follow-up literature
literature_findings.genotypes: rs61816761 (FLG R501X) or linked FLG locus genotype states
literature_findings.phenotype: Atopic dermatitis and ichthyosis vulgaris, Epidermal barrier dysfunction, Allergic sensitization and atopic asthma context
literature_findings.finding: The local curation treats rs61816761 (FLG R501X) as a research-grade FLG signal for allergic, airway, epithelial-barrier, or immune-inflammatory biology.
